histories: The grip is wrapped in leather so old it's become part of the handle.
Notches along the blade suggest it has seen real use — and real consequences.
Someone has scratched a name into the pommel, worn too smooth to read.
It's been repaired at least twice, and not by anyone particularly skilled.
quirks: It's very slightly heavier than it looks.
It always seems to be slightly colder than the air around it.
It makes no sound at all when drawn.
There's a faint smell of smoke about it that doesn't wash off.

histories: The lock is of a design no one in the party recognises.
It has been sealed with wax bearing a crest that has been partially scratched off.
The hinges are on the inside. There are no visible hinges on the outside.
It hums faintly when held — or perhaps that's just your hands.
quirks: Whatever is inside shifts when you tilt it, but makes no sound.
It is exactly the right size to be suspicious.
The wood is warm, as if it has been sitting in sunlight — it hasn't.
It smells faintly of a place none of you have been.

histories: The script engraved along its surface is in a language that predates the kingdom.
It has clearly been broken and reforged — the seam is almost invisible.
Every scholar who has examined it has disagreed about what it is.
It appears in the background of a painting in the city's civic hall.
quirks: Animals become quiet in its presence.
It casts no shadow in direct sunlight.
Those who touch it report dreaming of the same place that night.
It is always exactly at body temperature, regardless of conditions.

histories: The ink has run in places, but the important parts are still legible.
It has been folded and unfolded so many times the creases are near-tears.
Several lines have been struck through — forcefully, not neatly.
There are two different handwritings. They don't agree with each other.
quirks: The date given is either six years ago or six years from now, depending on the calendar.
A name has been removed so thoroughly it's left a hole.
The margins are filled with tiny, anxious annotations.
It smells of a specific perfume or pipe tobacco — someone would recognise it.

histories: The serial number has been ground off. Professionally.
Three different repair patches cover three different incidents no one discusses.
It's been recalibrated so many times the original settings are a memory.
A unit insignia is etched into the grip — the unit no longer exists.
quirks: It runs slightly hot. Not dangerously. Probably.
The safety indicator light hasn't worked in years. You check manually.
It makes a sound on startup that the manual says it shouldn't make.
Previous owners have left the grip worn smooth in exactly the right places.

histories: No manufacturer marking. No registry signature. Nothing to trace.
It's been wiped — but not as thoroughly as whoever wiped it believed.
The casing shows signs of having been opened and resealed multiple times.
It was found somewhere it has no obvious reason to be.
quirks: Standard readers don't recognise the format.
It draws slightly more power than its size suggests it should.
It has been accessed recently. Not by anyone in this room.
Whatever's on it, someone went to real effort to move it by hand rather than transmit it.

histories: It belongs to someone. Finding out who is either simple or complicated.
It's been reported stolen. The report was filed by the wrong person.
Someone kept this a long time before leaving it somewhere it would be found.
It changed hands at least twice recently. The second time wasn't voluntary.
quirks: The inscription on the back is in a handwriting that appears elsewhere in the case.
It's worth more than it looks, or less — hard to say which is worse.
The date on it doesn't line up with the story you've been told.
Somebody is going to ask about this before the night is over.

histories: It was found in a place where it should not have survived intact.
Three different people have tried to destroy it. None of them finished the attempt.
It has been left as an offering, an inheritance, and a warning at different points in its history.
Someone went to considerable trouble to conceal it. Different someone went to trouble to find it.
quirks: It is always slightly too cold to have been sitting in this room.
Reflective surfaces near it show something slightly different from what's actually there.
Those who hold it too long report the same dream.
It is heavier when carried toward certain places than others.
